nodes.name: Bird snare
nodes.notes: FLAG(kind): laying is an INVENTORY item option ('Bird snare' -> 'Lay'), not one of the 4 schema kinds; kind set to 'object' as best-fit, plugin likely needs inventory-item handling. FLAG(model): bird NPCs have NO 'Catch' option - you lay the snare, birds are trapped automatically, then Check the collapsed trap. 'All Bird Drops' mode cannot be attached to the lay action because the target bird is unknowable at lay time (same problem as chinchompa box traps). See report for per-bird card mapping (Crimson swift / Golden warbler / Tropical wagtail have creature cards; Copper longtail / Cerulean twitch do NOT).

nodes.name: Black warlock
nodes.notes: Net-only base mode = net group (magic net counts). 'With jar' mode adds the Butterfly jar group. 'All Butterfly Drops' mode would add this species' own creature card, but NO butterfly/moth creature card exists in Card.json (only '<name> mix' and, for moths, '<name> wing') - a per-species creature requirement is impossible; see report.

nodes.name: Box trap
nodes.notes: FLAG(kind): laying is an INVENTORY item option ('Box trap' -> 'Lay'), not one of the 4 schema kinds; kind set to 'object' as best-fit. DISCUSSION: a laid box trap cannot know which chinchompa will enter it, so an exact per-species card requirement is impossible at lay time. Proposed 'All Chinchompa Drops' mode uses a single any-of group of the three chin cards (grey chinchompa's caught item is the base 'Chinchompa' card). Alternative: key the species off the hunting area/region instead of the trap. Grey chinchompa == card 'Chinchompa'.

nodes.name: Baby impling
nodes.notes: Net-only mode = net group. 'Both' mode adds the Impling jar group (jar is mandatory in Puro-Puro, optional overworld). Barehanded catching (10 levels above net requirement) uses the SAME 'Catch' option, so the rule still keys on 'catch'. Per-impling '<name> impling jar' cards all exist if an 'all drops' mode is desired later.

nodes.name: Horned graahk
nodes.notes: Pitfall: tease the animal (option 'Tease') to lure it into the spiked pit; building the pit consumes Knife + any logs (redwood/arctic pine excluded). Items-only base mode = Teasing stick group AND Knife group AND logs group (any-of the 8 standard firemaking logs). 'All' mode adds the animal's own creature card group. AXE IS OPTIONAL (only cuts nearby trees for extra logs) so it is intentionally NOT required - see report for the axe card list if the owner still wants it. Larupia & Kyatt are also pitfall/deadfall creatures but have NO creature card, so they are excluded.

nodes.name: Guild Hunter Aco
nodes.notes: expert-tier rumour master. Requires ALL cards for every assignable creature that HAS a card. Excluded (no matching card in Card.json): Sabre-toothed kebbit, Sabre-toothed kyatt, Moonlight moth. Judgment mappings used: Spotted kebbit->'Spotted kebbit fur', Grey chinchompa->'Chinchompa', Pyre fox->'Raw pyre fox' (caught items).

nodes.name: Young tree
nodes.notes: Net-trap object is 'Young tree', option 'Set-trap'; each salamander area has its own Young tree - node distinguished by the 'Swamp lizard' add-on. Rope+Net base mode = Rope group AND Small fishing net group. 'Items+Sally' mode adds the salamander's own card group. Object name for the Varlamore Tecu salamander should be re-verified in-game.

nodes.name: Air altar
nodes.notes: Standard elemental/catalytic altar.

nodes.name: Altar
nodes.notes: Ourania (ZMI) altar. No talisman needed; crafts RANDOM runes (level-based), so no specific rune group. Pure or daeyalt essence only. Object name is generic 'Altar' (distinct from the renamed 'X altar' objects).

nodes.name: Astral altar
nodes.notes: Requires pure (or daeyalt) essence; rune essence cannot craft this rune. No talisman/tiara needed for access (favour/quest-gated); talisman group omitted.

nodes.name: Blood altar
